title: Perform Daily Preventive Inspection Of Each Hospital Station
summary: Inspect each hospital station as part of daily preventive maintenance to verify the barcode scanner is operational, the stacklight is operational, and the tabletop is free of debris.
when_to_use: Use during the documented daily preventive maintenance inspection for each hospital station.
not_for: Corrective repair of a failed barcode scanner
Corrective repair of a failed stacklight
Any cleaning or corrective method not explicitly supported by the source

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the documented daily preventive inspection of each hospital station and escalate issues when inspection items are not satisfactory.

safety_notes: This source provides inspection points only and does not provide corrective maintenance actions.
Do not perform unsupported repair, setup, or adjustment actions based on this runbook alone.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access to each hospital station
Visual access to the barcode scanner, stacklight, and tabletop

system_preconditions: Hospital station is available for visual inspection

visual_references.description: Hospital station HMI Operations screen showing status and barcode-related fields relevant to hospital station operation context.
Hospital HMI screen associated with barcode no-read handling; useful as contextual visual support for hospital station barcode-related operation.
Hospital HMI screen associated with barcode mismatch handling; useful as contextual visual support for hospital station barcode-related operation.
Hospital HMI screen showing barcode scan workflow context using the green scanner.
Hospital station operation artifact showing tote handling context and scanner use at the hospital station.

steps.title: Go to each hospital station
steps.purpose: Ensure every hospital station is included in the daily preventive inspection scope.
steps.instruction: Go to each hospital station and prepare to inspect the documented daily preventive maintenance items at that station.
steps.expected_result: The inspector is physically present at a hospital station and ready to verify the listed inspection points.
steps.healthy_condition: Each hospital station is reached and included in the inspection round.
steps.failure_condition: A hospital station is not inspected or cannot be accessed for inspection.
stop_or_escalate_if: A hospital station cannot be accessed for inspection
The inspection cannot be completed for every hospital station

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 7.1.2: HOSPITAL STATION Inspect each hospital station for the following: The barcode scanner is operational. The stacklight is operational. The tabletop is free of debris.

steps.title: Verify barcode scanner is operational
steps.purpose: Confirm the hospital station barcode scanner meets the daily preventive inspection requirement.
steps.instruction: Verify that the barcode scanner at the hospital station is operational. Use only source-supported observation and context; the source does not define a detailed test method in this maintenance section.
steps.expected_result: The barcode scanner is confirmed operational for the hospital station.
steps.failure_condition: The barcode scanner is not operational or its operational state cannot be confirmed from the available source-supported inspection.

actions.label: Inspect barcode scanner status
actions.exact_action: Verify that the barcode scanner is operational.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Barcode scan workflow context showing use of the green scanner and barcode/Tote ID population.
Hospital station barcode-related screen context for no-read conditions.
Hospital station barcode-related screen context for mismatch conditions.
Hospital station operation context describing scanning the tote barcode using the green scanner.

stop_or_escalate_if: The barcode scanner is not operational
The barcode scanner operational state cannot be confirmed using source-supported inspection

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 7.1.2: HOSPITAL STATION Inspect each hospital station for the following: The barcode scanner is operational.
At the hospital station, an operator uses a green scanner to determine why a tote was brought to the station.

steps.title: Verify stacklight is operational
steps.purpose: Confirm the hospital station stacklight meets the daily preventive inspection requirement.
steps.instruction: Verify that the stacklight at the hospital station is operational. The source identifies this as an inspection point but does not provide a detailed test method.
steps.expected_result: The stacklight is confirmed operational for the hospital station.
steps.failure_condition: The stacklight is not operational or its operational state cannot be confirmed from the available source-supported inspection.

actions.label: Inspect stacklight status
actions.exact_action: Verify that the stacklight is operational.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital HMI screen used when a tote bar code cannot be read at the Operator Station and must be handled manually at the Hospital Station.

stop_or_escalate_if: The stacklight operational state cannot be confirmed using source-supported inspection

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 7.1.2: HOSPITAL STATION Inspect each hospital station for the following: The stacklight is operational.

steps.title: Inspect tabletop for debris
steps.purpose: Confirm the hospital station tabletop meets the daily preventive inspection cleanliness requirement.
steps.instruction: Inspect the tabletop and confirm it is free of debris.
steps.expected_result: The tabletop is confirmed free of debris.
steps.healthy_condition: The tabletop is free of debris.
steps.failure_condition: Debris is present on the tabletop or the tabletop cannot be restored to a debris-free condition using source-approved methods.

actions.label: Inspect tabletop cleanliness
actions.exact_action: Inspect the tabletop and confirm it is free of debris.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tabletop is not free of debris
Debris cannot be cleared using source-approved methods

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 7.1.2: HOSPITAL STATION Inspect each hospital station for the following: The tabletop is free of debris.

success_criteria: Each hospital station has been inspected.
Each hospital station barcode scanner is operational.
Each hospital station stacklight is operational.
Each hospital station tabletop is free of debris.
healthy_conditions: Barcode scanner is operational at each hospital station.
Stacklight is operational at each hospital station.
Tabletop is free of debris at each hospital station.
failure_conditions: Any hospital station barcode scanner is not operational.
Any hospital station stacklight is not operational.
Any hospital station tabletop contains debris.
Any hospital station cannot be inspected.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the barcode scanner is not operational.
Escalate if the stacklight is not operational.
Escalate if tabletop debris cannot be cleared using source-approved methods.
Escalate when corrective action is required because this source provides inspection points only and does not provide corrective actions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Green scanner and barcode/Tote ID workflow context at the hospital station.
Hospital station barcode exception screen referenced as image support in the candidate.
Hospital station barcode mismatch screen for barcode-related context.
Hospital station operations screen showing AGV, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status context.
Hospital station operation context showing scanner use and tote handling.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: 7.1.2: HOSPITAL STATION Inspect each hospital station for the following: The barcode scanner is operational. The stacklight is operational. The tabletop is free of debris.

missing_details: The source does not define a detailed method to test barcode scanner operation during daily preventive maintenance.
The source does not define a detailed method to test stacklight operation during daily preventive maintenance.
The source does not provide corrective actions for failed inspection items.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required for this inspection.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
